title: JACOMB LLC EV Charging at Progress Park -- Equity in Battery Storage Contracting
summary: Council votes on item 46 (JACOMB LLC agreement for EV charging stations and battery storage grants at Progress Park). Councilor Majok urges fairness for local small businesses entering the battery/EV space, warning against excluding local operators in favor of larger outside companies. Council President Paniagua echoes concern about waiving RFP processes, noting this has denied Black-owned, LGBTQ, and union businesses bidding opportunities. A third councilor notes these are the first publicly-accessible Level 3 EV chargers in Syracuse and frames the investment in climate goals and park usability. Item passes unanimously. Helen Hudson, newly appointed at-large councilor, recognized in chamber but declines to speak.

department_refs: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs

political_signal.description: Majok and Paniagua raise equity concerns about RFP waivers in emerging battery/EV sector, urging local and minority-owned businesses receive fair contracting opportunities

processing_notes: Dept of Permits and Development detection is false positive. JACOMB LLC working with Division of Planning & Sustainability. Two grants: NYS DEC Climate Smart Communities ZEV grant ($270K) and National Grid Battery Ready Program ($165K). Helen Hudson appointed earlier in meeting (seg 1) and is present but declines to speak. Rules waived for this item by Monto and Nave.
Segment 10 of 10 from Common Council Regular Meeting, Monday February 23rd 2026
